Suite TL;DR: The BC's upstream ptp4l instance (ptp4l.1.config / ens2f0np0) is stuck in MASTER state rather than SLAVE, blocking clock synchronization entirely, compounded by a broken pmc fallback command introduced in a June 2026 ptp-operator commit.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Broken pmc command args in getClockIDViaPMC fallback (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

grandmasterIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

• Root Cause: Commit 3b93c9f (Jun 11, 2026, "tests: add retries to PTP clock ID lookups") introduced getClockIDViaPMC in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 which passes "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" as two separate arguments to pmc via pods.ExecCommand. In pmc's batch mode, each argv[optind++] is fed independently to pmc_do_command() (see pmc_common.c:1070), which calls sscanf(str, " %10s %64s", action_str, id_str). Passing "GET" alone returns only 1 match (not 2), so pmc_do_command returns -1 and prints "bad command: GET". Then "PARENT_DATA_SET" alone similarly produces "bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET". The correct invocation passes "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single argument so that sscanf parses both tokens in one call. The log-based lookup (1-minute timeout) fails first because the BC is in FREERUN, triggering this broken fallback.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/3b93c9f408647a8b55d37920103c57a3e2cb11c2 (ptphelper.go:144 — []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} must be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"})
• Is it a flake? No — deterministic, every run where log parsing falls back to pmc will fail identically
• Suggested Fix: Change the ExecCommand call in getClockIDViaPMC to pass "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single slice element, matching the working pattern already used in ptptesthelper.go:588 ([]string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}).

• Group 2: BC ptp4l.1.config stuck in FREERUN / ens2f0np0 in MASTER (affects: "Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on dual NIC boundary clock", "PTP metric is present verifies on slave", "Should receive events after linuxptp-daemon pod restart", "The slave node network interface is taken down and up", "BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage")
• Evidence:

```

Expected PMC clock class 6 for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config

Expected : 248 to equal : 6


incorrect role for ens2f0np0, role expected=1(SLAVE), role observed=2(MASTER)


Time metrics are not detected


timedout PushInitialValue, waiting for log in ns=ptp-event-framework-test pod=consumer,

looking for = Got CurrentState: ({.*})

```

From the linuxptp-daemon log on cnfdf32 (the BC node):

```

ptp4l[22542.578]: [ptp4l.0.config:5] port 1 (eno12399np0): LISTENING to MASTER on ANNOUNCE_RECEIPT_TIMEOUT_EXPIRES

ptp4l[22542.578]: [ptp4l.0.config:5] selected local clock b49691.fffe.cc0964 as best master

```

— No ptp4l.1.config entries present in the cnfdf32 daemon log spanning the entire test window.

• Root Cause: The BC's upstream ptp4l instance (ptp4l.1.config / ens2f0np0 on cnfdf32) never reaches SLAVE state because it does not receive PTP Announce messages from the GM, causing ANNOUNCE_RECEIPT_TIMEOUT_EXPIRES and self-promotion to MASTER. Clock class therefore stays at 248 (FREERUN). The absence of ptp4l.1.config entries in the cnfdf32 daemon log (7792-line, 976 KB log spanning the full test window) despite ptp4l.0.config running normally on the same pod suggests the BC's second ptp4l instance either did not start or was never configured to start. The most likely candidate is a regression in linuxptp-daemon main branch commit fd6ff1e (PR #617, "Sync from upstream 30-Jun-2026"), which added new PMCProcess management code in pkg/daemon/pmc.go (328 net-new lines) that could interfere with multi-config BC startup sequencing — though the exact daemon-side mechanism cannot be pinpointed without the cnfdf31 full log. The same symptom (ens2f0np0 in MASTER, clockClass 248) reproduces identically in the dualnicbcha suite on the same run, indicating a systemic BC configuration failure rather than a transient flake.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/pull/617 (fd6ff1e) — "Sync from upstream 2026-06-30" on main branch. Needs further investigation with full cnfdf31 daemon logs. Verified main-branch-only (check_backport would need to confirm absence on release-5.0); however, since USE_UPSTREAM=true this run, images are built from main directly.
• Is it a flake? No — persists for the entire suite duration (>1 hour) and reproduces across two back-to-back suites (dualnicbc and dualnicbcha)
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the new PMCProcess.CmdRun goroutine in pkg/daemon/pmc.go (introduced in PR #617) interferes with BC's dual ptp4l instance startup. Check daemon logs on the BC node during config deployment for any panic, socket error, or race condition preventing ptp4l.1.config from starting.

• Group 3: phc2sys SIGTERM test — new test failure (affects: "Should restart phc2sys with no socket errors after SIGTERM")
• Evidence:

```

Expected phc2sys process status to be 1 for GM

Expected : 0 to equal : 1

Timed out after 180.001s

```

• Root Cause: phc2sys process count is 0 (not running) when the test expects 1 (running after SIGTERM-triggered restart). This test was added in ptp-operator PR #251 (Jul 1, 2026, "tests-cleanup-ptp-socket-and-processes"). Since the BC is not in LOCKED state (Group 2), phc2sys may not be started by the daemon in the first place, making the SIGTERM-and-count test impossible to pass. It is also possible the new test itself has a timing or node-targeting bug.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/459caf2a4caca4da18bad618e0674eee1f5b4044 (PR #251) and/or the Group 2 daemon regression
• Is it a flake? No
• Suggested Fix: Fix Group 2 (BC sync) first; if the test still fails with a synced BC, inspe



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

```

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/alias.GetAlias, neither of which maps it to the actual slave interface, so openshift_ptp_clock_state is labeled iface="master". The old extractRegularMetrics() code (metrics.go:473–475) had explicit logic to resolve "master" to the tracked slave interface alias via masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias. That resolution is absent from the new path.

• Breaking PR/Commit: fd6ff1e — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/commit/fd6ff1e2dc9c651a96a85a976da01498fa0b3417 (Merge PR #617 "upstream-sync-2026-06-30", merged 2026-06-30). The specific bug lives in the newly created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics().

• Is it a flake? No — the metric label regression is deterministic on any run using the main branch of linuxptp-daemon after this merge.

• Suggested Fix: In processParsedMetrics() for the ptp4lProcessName case, when ptpMetrics.Iface resolves to "master", fall back to masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias before calling updateClockStateMetrics — mirroring the logic in the old extractRegularMetrics() at metrics.go:473. Also remove (or guard) the masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) call on line 118 when Iface is "master" to prevent corrupting the tracked slave interface.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (10 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The BC's upstream ptp4l instance (ptp4l.1.config / ens2f0np0) is stuck in MASTER state rather than SLAVE, blocking clock synchronization entirely, compounded by a broken pmc fallback command introduced in a June 2026 ptp-operator commit.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Broken pmc command args in getClockIDViaPMC fallback (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

grandmasterIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

• Root Cause: Commit 3b93c9f (Jun 11, 2026, "tests: add retries to PTP clock ID lookups") introduced getClockIDViaPMC in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 which passes "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" as two separate arguments to pmc via pods.ExecCommand. In pmc's batch mode, each argv[optind++] is fed independently to pmc_do_command() (see pmc_common.c:1070), which calls sscanf(str, " %10s %64s", action_str, id_str). Passing "GET" alone returns only 1 match (not 2), so pmc_do_command returns -1 and prints "bad command: GET". Then "PARENT_DATA_SET" alone similarly produces "bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET". The correct invocation passes "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single argument so that sscanf parses both tokens in one call. The log-based lookup (1-minute timeout) fails first because the BC is in FREERUN, triggering this broken fallback.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/3b93c9f408647a8b55d37920103c57a3e2cb11c2 (ptphelper.go:144 — []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} must be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"})
• Is it a flake? No — deterministic, every run where log parsing falls back to pmc will fail identically
• Suggested Fix: Change the ExecCommand call in getClockIDViaPMC to pass "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single slice element, matching the working pattern already used in ptptesthelper.go:588 ([]string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}).

• Group 2: BC ptp4l.1.config stuck in FREERUN / ens2f0np0 in MASTER (affects: "Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on dual NIC boundary clock", "PTP metric is present verifies on slave", "Should receive events after linuxptp-daemon pod restart", "The slave node network interface is taken down and up", "BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage")
• Evidence:

```

Expected PMC clock class 6 for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config

Expected : 248 to equal : 6


incorrect role for ens2f0np0, role expected=1(SLAVE), role observed=2(MASTER)


Time metrics are not detected


timedout PushInitialValue, waiting for log in ns=ptp-event-framework-test pod=consumer,

looking for = Got CurrentState: ({.*})

```

From the linuxptp-daemon log on cnfdf32 (the BC node):

```

ptp4l[22542.578]: [ptp4l.0.config:5] port 1 (eno12399np0): LISTENING to MASTER on ANNOUNCE_RECEIPT_TIMEOUT_EXPIRES

ptp4l[22542.578]: [ptp4l.0.config:5] selected local clock b49691.fffe.cc0964 as best master

```

— No ptp4l.1.config entries present in the cnfdf32 daemon log spanning the entire test window.

• Root Cause: The BC's upstream ptp4l instance (ptp4l.1.config / ens2f0np0 on cnfdf32) never reaches SLAVE state because it does not receive PTP Announce messages from the GM, causing ANNOUNCE_RECEIPT_TIMEOUT_EXPIRES and self-promotion to MASTER. Clock class therefore stays at 248 (FREERUN). The absence of ptp4l.1.config entries in the cnfdf32 daemon log (7792-line, 976 KB log spanning the full test window) despite ptp4l.0.config running normally on the same pod suggests the BC's second ptp4l instance either did not start or was never configured to start. The most likely candidate is a regression in linuxptp-daemon main branch commit fd6ff1e (PR #617, "Sync from upstream 30-Jun-2026"), which added new PMCProcess management code in pkg/daemon/pmc.go (328 net-new lines) that could interfere with multi-config BC startup sequencing — though the exact daemon-side mechanism cannot be pinpointed without the cnfdf31 full log. The same symptom (ens2f0np0 in MASTER, clockClass 248) reproduces identically in the dualnicbcha suite on the same run, indicating a systemic BC configuration failure rather than a transient flake.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/pull/617 (fd6ff1e) — "Sync from upstream 2026-06-30" on main branch. Needs further investigation with full cnfdf31 daemon logs. Verified main-branch-only (check_backport would need to confirm absence on release-5.0); however, since USE_UPSTREAM=true this run, images are built from main directly.
• Is it a flake? No — persists for the entire suite duration (>1 hour) and reproduces across two back-to-back suites (dualnicbc and dualnicbcha)
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the new PMCProcess.CmdRun goroutine in pkg/daemon/pmc.go (introduced in PR #617) interferes with BC's dual ptp4l instance startup. Check daemon logs on the BC node during config deployment for any panic, socket error, or race condition preventing ptp4l.1.config from starting.

• Group 3: phc2sys SIGTERM test — new test failure (affects: "Should restart phc2sys with no socket errors after SIGTERM")
• Evidence:

```

Expected phc2sys process status to be 1 for GM

Expected : 0 to equal : 1

Timed out after 180.001s

```

• Root Cause: phc2sys process count is 0 (not running) when the test expects 1 (running after SIGTERM-triggered restart). This test was added in ptp-operator PR #251 (Jul 1, 2026, "tests-cleanup-ptp-socket-and-processes"). Since the BC is not in LOCKED state (Group 2), phc2sys may not be started by the daemon in the first place, making the SIGTERM-and-count test impossible to pass. It is also possible the new test itself has a timing or node-targeting bug.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/459caf2a4caca4da18bad618e0674eee1f5b4044 (PR #251) and/or the Group 2 daemon regression
• Is it a flake? No
• Suggested Fix: Fix Group 2 (BC sync) first; if the test still fails with a synced BC, inspe



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

```

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/alias.GetAlias, neither of which maps it to the actual slave interface, so openshift_ptp_clock_state is labeled iface="master". The old extractRegularMetrics() code (metrics.go:473–475) had explicit logic to resolve "master" to the tracked slave interface alias via masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias. That resolution is absent from the new path.

• Breaking PR/Commit: fd6ff1e — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/commit/fd6ff1e2dc9c651a96a85a976da01498fa0b3417 (Merge PR #617 "upstream-sync-2026-06-30", merged 2026-06-30). The specific bug lives in the newly created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics().

• Is it a flake? No — the metric label regression is deterministic on any run using the main branch of linuxptp-daemon after this merge.

• Suggested Fix: In processParsedMetrics() for the ptp4lProcessName case, when ptpMetrics.Iface resolves to "master", fall back to masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias before calling updateClockStateMetrics — mirroring the logic in the old extractRegularMetrics() at metrics.go:473. Also remove (or guard) the masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) call on line 118 when Iface is "master" to prevent corrupting the tracked slave interface.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (10 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The BC's upstream ptp4l instance (ptp4l.1.config / ens2f0np0) is stuck in MASTER state rather than SLAVE, blocking clock synchronization entirely, compounded by a broken pmc fallback command introduced in a June 2026 ptp-operator commit.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Broken pmc command args in getClockIDViaPMC fallback (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

grandmasterIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

• Root Cause: Commit 3b93c9f (Jun 11, 2026, "tests: add retries to PTP clock ID lookups") introduced getClockIDViaPMC in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 which passes "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" as two separate arguments to pmc via pods.ExecCommand. In pmc's batch mode, each argv[optind++] is fed independently to pmc_do_command() (see pmc_common.c:1070), which calls sscanf(str, " %10s %64s", action_str, id_str). Passing "GET" alone returns only 1 match (not 2), so pmc_do_command returns -1 and prints "bad command: GET". Then "PARENT_DATA_SET" alone similarly produces "bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET". The correct invocation passes "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single argument so that sscanf parses both tokens in one call. The log-based lookup (1-minute timeout) fails first because the BC is in FREERUN, triggering this broken fallback.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/3b93c9f408647a8b55d37920103c57a3e2cb11c2 (ptphelper.go:144 — []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} must be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"})
• Is it a flake? No — deterministic, every run where log parsing falls back to pmc will fail identically
• Suggested Fix: Change the ExecCommand call in getClockIDViaPMC to pass "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single slice element, matching the working pattern already used in ptptesthelper.go:588 ([]string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}).

• Group 2: BC ptp4l.1.config stuck in FREERUN / ens2f0np0 in MASTER (affects: "Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on dual NIC boundary clock", "PTP metric is present verifies on slave", "Should receive events after linuxptp-daemon pod restart", "The slave node network interface is taken down and up", "BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage")
• Evidence:

```

Expected PMC clock class 6 for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config

Expected : 248 to equal : 6


incorrect role for ens2f0np0, role expected=1(SLAVE), role observed=2(MASTER)


Time metrics are not detected


timedout PushInitialValue, waiting for log in ns=ptp-event-framework-test pod=consumer,

looking for = Got CurrentState: ({.*})

```

From the linuxptp-daemon log on cnfdf32 (the BC node):

```

ptp4l[22542.578]: [ptp4l.0.config:5] port 1 (eno12399np0): LISTENING to MASTER on ANNOUNCE_RECEIPT_TIMEOUT_EXPIRES

ptp4l[22542.578]: [ptp4l.0.config:5] selected local clock b49691.fffe.cc0964 as best master

```

— No ptp4l.1.config entries present in the cnfdf32 daemon log spanning the entire test window.

• Root Cause: The BC's upstream ptp4l instance (ptp4l.1.config / ens2f0np0 on cnfdf32) never reaches SLAVE state because it does not receive PTP Announce messages from the GM, causing ANNOUNCE_RECEIPT_TIMEOUT_EXPIRES and self-promotion to MASTER. Clock class therefore stays at 248 (FREERUN). The absence of ptp4l.1.config entries in the cnfdf32 daemon log (7792-line, 976 KB log spanning the full test window) despite ptp4l.0.config running normally on the same pod suggests the BC's second ptp4l instance either did not start or was never configured to start. The most likely candidate is a regression in linuxptp-daemon main branch commit fd6ff1e (PR #617, "Sync from upstream 30-Jun-2026"), which added new PMCProcess management code in pkg/daemon/pmc.go (328 net-new lines) that could interfere with multi-config BC startup sequencing — though the exact daemon-side mechanism cannot be pinpointed without the cnfdf31 full log. The same symptom (ens2f0np0 in MASTER, clockClass 248) reproduces identically in the dualnicbcha suite on the same run, indicating a systemic BC configuration failure rather than a transient flake.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/pull/617 (fd6ff1e) — "Sync from upstream 2026-06-30" on main branch. Needs further investigation with full cnfdf31 daemon logs. Verified main-branch-only (check_backport would need to confirm absence on release-5.0); however, since USE_UPSTREAM=true this run, images are built from main directly.
• Is it a flake? No — persists for the entire suite duration (>1 hour) and reproduces across two back-to-back suites (dualnicbc and dualnicbcha)
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the new PMCProcess.CmdRun goroutine in pkg/daemon/pmc.go (introduced in PR #617) interferes with BC's dual ptp4l instance startup. Check daemon logs on the BC node during config deployment for any panic, socket error, or race condition preventing ptp4l.1.config from starting.

• Group 3: phc2sys SIGTERM test — new test failure (affects: "Should restart phc2sys with no socket errors after SIGTERM")
• Evidence:

```

Expected phc2sys process status to be 1 for GM

Expected : 0 to equal : 1

Timed out after 180.001s

```

• Root Cause: phc2sys process count is 0 (not running) when the test expects 1 (running after SIGTERM-triggered restart). This test was added in ptp-operator PR #251 (Jul 1, 2026, "tests-cleanup-ptp-socket-and-processes"). Since the BC is not in LOCKED state (Group 2), phc2sys may not be started by the daemon in the first place, making the SIGTERM-and-count test impossible to pass. It is also possible the new test itself has a timing or node-targeting bug.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/459caf2a4caca4da18bad618e0674eee1f5b4044 (PR #251) and/or the Group 2 daemon regression
• Is it a flake? No
• Suggested Fix: Fix Group 2 (BC sync) first; if the test still fails with a synced BC, inspe



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

```

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/alias.GetAlias, neither of which maps it to the actual slave interface, so openshift_ptp_clock_state is labeled iface="master". The old extractRegularMetrics() code (metrics.go:473–475) had explicit logic to resolve "master" to the tracked slave interface alias via masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias. That resolution is absent from the new path.

• Breaking PR/Commit: fd6ff1e — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/commit/fd6ff1e2dc9c651a96a85a976da01498fa0b3417 (Merge PR #617 "upstream-sync-2026-06-30", merged 2026-06-30). The specific bug lives in the newly created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics().

• Is it a flake? No — the metric label regression is deterministic on any run using the main branch of linuxptp-daemon after this merge.

• Suggested Fix: In processParsedMetrics() for the ptp4lProcessName case, when ptpMetrics.Iface resolves to "master", fall back to masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias before calling updateClockStateMetrics — mirroring the logic in the old extractRegularMetrics() at metrics.go:473. Also remove (or guard) the masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) call on line 118 when Iface is "master" to prevent corrupting the tracked slave interface.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (10 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The BC's upstream ptp4l instance (ptp4l.1.config / ens2f0np0) is stuck in MASTER state rather than SLAVE, blocking clock synchronization entirely, compounded by a broken pmc fallback command introduced in a June 2026 ptp-operator commit.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Broken pmc command args in getClockIDViaPMC fallback (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

grandmasterIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

• Root Cause: Commit 3b93c9f (Jun 11, 2026, "tests: add retries to PTP clock ID lookups") introduced getClockIDViaPMC in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 which passes "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" as two separate arguments to pmc via pods.ExecCommand. In pmc's batch mode, each argv[optind++] is fed independently to pmc_do_command() (see pmc_common.c:1070), which calls sscanf(str, " %10s %64s", action_str, id_str). Passing "GET" alone returns only 1 match (not 2), so pmc_do_command returns -1 and prints "bad command: GET". Then "PARENT_DATA_SET" alone similarly produces "bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET". The correct invocation passes "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single argument so that sscanf parses both tokens in one call. The log-based lookup (1-minute timeout) fails first because the BC is in FREERUN, triggering this broken fallback.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/3b93c9f408647a8b55d37920103c57a3e2cb11c2 (ptphelper.go:144 — []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} must be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"})
• Is it a flake? No — deterministic, every run where log parsing falls back to pmc will fail identically
• Suggested Fix: Change the ExecCommand call in getClockIDViaPMC to pass "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single slice element, matching the working pattern already used in ptptesthelper.go:588 ([]string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}).

• Group 2: BC ptp4l.1.config stuck in FREERUN / ens2f0np0 in MASTER (affects: "Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on dual NIC boundary clock", "PTP metric is present verifies on slave", "Should receive events after linuxptp-daemon pod restart", "The slave node network interface is taken down and up", "BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage")
• Evidence:

```

Expected PMC clock class 6 for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config

Expected : 248 to equal : 6


incorrect role for ens2f0np0, role expected=1(SLAVE), role observed=2(MASTER)


Time metrics are not detected


timedout PushInitialValue, waiting for log in ns=ptp-event-framework-test pod=consumer,

looking for = Got CurrentState: ({.*})

```

From the linuxptp-daemon log on cnfdf32 (the BC node):

```

ptp4l[22542.578]: [ptp4l.0.config:5] port 1 (eno12399np0): LISTENING to MASTER on ANNOUNCE_RECEIPT_TIMEOUT_EXPIRES

ptp4l[22542.578]: [ptp4l.0.config:5] selected local clock b49691.fffe.cc0964 as best master

```

— No ptp4l.1.config entries present in the cnfdf32 daemon log spanning the entire test window.

• Root Cause: The BC's upstream ptp4l instance (ptp4l.1.config / ens2f0np0 on cnfdf32) never reaches SLAVE state because it does not receive PTP Announce messages from the GM, causing ANNOUNCE_RECEIPT_TIMEOUT_EXPIRES and self-promotion to MASTER. Clock class therefore stays at 248 (FREERUN). The absence of ptp4l.1.config entries in the cnfdf32 daemon log (7792-line, 976 KB log spanning the full test window) despite ptp4l.0.config running normally on the same pod suggests the BC's second ptp4l instance either did not start or was never configured to start. The most likely candidate is a regression in linuxptp-daemon main branch commit fd6ff1e (PR #617, "Sync from upstream 30-Jun-2026"), which added new PMCProcess management code in pkg/daemon/pmc.go (328 net-new lines) that could interfere with multi-config BC startup sequencing — though the exact daemon-side mechanism cannot be pinpointed without the cnfdf31 full log. The same symptom (ens2f0np0 in MASTER, clockClass 248) reproduces identically in the dualnicbcha suite on the same run, indicating a systemic BC configuration failure rather than a transient flake.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/pull/617 (fd6ff1e) — "Sync from upstream 2026-06-30" on main branch. Needs further investigation with full cnfdf31 daemon logs. Verified main-branch-only (check_backport would need to confirm absence on release-5.0); however, since USE_UPSTREAM=true this run, images are built from main directly.
• Is it a flake? No — persists for the entire suite duration (>1 hour) and reproduces across two back-to-back suites (dualnicbc and dualnicbcha)
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the new PMCProcess.CmdRun goroutine in pkg/daemon/pmc.go (introduced in PR #617) interferes with BC's dual ptp4l instance startup. Check daemon logs on the BC node during config deployment for any panic, socket error, or race condition preventing ptp4l.1.config from starting.

• Group 3: phc2sys SIGTERM test — new test failure (affects: "Should restart phc2sys with no socket errors after SIGTERM")
• Evidence:

```

Expected phc2sys process status to be 1 for GM

Expected : 0 to equal : 1

Timed out after 180.001s

```

• Root Cause: phc2sys process count is 0 (not running) when the test expects 1 (running after SIGTERM-triggered restart). This test was added in ptp-operator PR #251 (Jul 1, 2026, "tests-cleanup-ptp-socket-and-processes"). Since the BC is not in LOCKED state (Group 2), phc2sys may not be started by the daemon in the first place, making the SIGTERM-and-count test impossible to pass. It is also possible the new test itself has a timing or node-targeting bug.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/459caf2a4caca4da18bad618e0674eee1f5b4044 (PR #251) and/or the Group 2 daemon regression
• Is it a flake? No
• Suggested Fix: Fix Group 2 (BC sync) first; if the test still fails with a synced BC, inspe



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

```

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/alias.GetAlias, neither of which maps it to the actual slave interface, so openshift_ptp_clock_state is labeled iface="master". The old extractRegularMetrics() code (metrics.go:473–475) had explicit logic to resolve "master" to the tracked slave interface alias via masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias. That resolution is absent from the new path.

• Breaking PR/Commit: fd6ff1e — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/commit/fd6ff1e2dc9c651a96a85a976da01498fa0b3417 (Merge PR #617 "upstream-sync-2026-06-30", merged 2026-06-30). The specific bug lives in the newly created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics().

• Is it a flake? No — the metric label regression is deterministic on any run using the main branch of linuxptp-daemon after this merge.

• Suggested Fix: In processParsedMetrics() for the ptp4lProcessName case, when ptpMetrics.Iface resolves to "master", fall back to masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias before calling updateClockStateMetrics — mirroring the logic in the old extractRegularMetrics() at metrics.go:473. Also remove (or guard) the masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) call on line 118 when Iface is "master" to prevent corrupting the tracked slave interface.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (10 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The BC's upstream ptp4l instance (ptp4l.1.config / ens2f0np0) is stuck in MASTER state rather than SLAVE, blocking clock synchronization entirely, compounded by a broken pmc fallback command introduced in a June 2026 ptp-operator commit.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Broken pmc command args in getClockIDViaPMC fallback (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

grandmasterIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

• Root Cause: Commit 3b93c9f (Jun 11, 2026, "tests: add retries to PTP clock ID lookups") introduced getClockIDViaPMC in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 which passes "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" as two separate arguments to pmc via pods.ExecCommand. In pmc's batch mode, each argv[optind++] is fed independently to pmc_do_command() (see pmc_common.c:1070), which calls sscanf(str, " %10s %64s", action_str, id_str). Passing "GET" alone returns only 1 match (not 2), so pmc_do_command returns -1 and prints "bad command: GET". Then "PARENT_DATA_SET" alone similarly produces "bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET". The correct invocation passes "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single argument so that sscanf parses both tokens in one call. The log-based lookup (1-minute timeout) fails first because the BC is in FREERUN, triggering this broken fallback.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/3b93c9f408647a8b55d37920103c57a3e2cb11c2 (ptphelper.go:144 — []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} must be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"})
• Is it a flake? No — deterministic, every run where log parsing falls back to pmc will fail identically
• Suggested Fix: Change the ExecCommand call in getClockIDViaPMC to pass "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single slice element, matching the working pattern already used in ptptesthelper.go:588 ([]string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}).

• Group 2: BC ptp4l.1.config stuck in FREERUN / ens2f0np0 in MASTER (affects: "Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on dual NIC boundary clock", "PTP metric is present verifies on slave", "Should receive events after linuxptp-daemon pod restart", "The slave node network interface is taken down and up", "BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage")
• Evidence:

```

Expected PMC clock class 6 for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config

Expected : 248 to equal : 6


incorrect role for ens2f0np0, role expected=1(SLAVE), role observed=2(MASTER)


Time metrics are not detected


timedout PushInitialValue, waiting for log in ns=ptp-event-framework-test pod=consumer,

looking for = Got CurrentState: ({.*})

```

From the linuxptp-daemon log on cnfdf32 (the BC node):

```

ptp4l[22542.578]: [ptp4l.0.config:5] port 1 (eno12399np0): LISTENING to MASTER on ANNOUNCE_RECEIPT_TIMEOUT_EXPIRES

ptp4l[22542.578]: [ptp4l.0.config:5] selected local clock b49691.fffe.cc0964 as best master

```

— No ptp4l.1.config entries present in the cnfdf32 daemon log spanning the entire test window.

• Root Cause: The BC's upstream ptp4l instance (ptp4l.1.config / ens2f0np0 on cnfdf32) never reaches SLAVE state because it does not receive PTP Announce messages from the GM, causing ANNOUNCE_RECEIPT_TIMEOUT_EXPIRES and self-promotion to MASTER. Clock class therefore stays at 248 (FREERUN). The absence of ptp4l.1.config entries in the cnfdf32 daemon log (7792-line, 976 KB log spanning the full test window) despite ptp4l.0.config running normally on the same pod suggests the BC's second ptp4l instance either did not start or was never configured to start. The most likely candidate is a regression in linuxptp-daemon main branch commit fd6ff1e (PR #617, "Sync from upstream 30-Jun-2026"), which added new PMCProcess management code in pkg/daemon/pmc.go (328 net-new lines) that could interfere with multi-config BC startup sequencing — though the exact daemon-side mechanism cannot be pinpointed without the cnfdf31 full log. The same symptom (ens2f0np0 in MASTER, clockClass 248) reproduces identically in the dualnicbcha suite on the same run, indicating a systemic BC configuration failure rather than a transient flake.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/pull/617 (fd6ff1e) — "Sync from upstream 2026-06-30" on main branch. Needs further investigation with full cnfdf31 daemon logs. Verified main-branch-only (check_backport would need to confirm absence on release-5.0); however, since USE_UPSTREAM=true this run, images are built from main directly.
• Is it a flake? No — persists for the entire suite duration (>1 hour) and reproduces across two back-to-back suites (dualnicbc and dualnicbcha)
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the new PMCProcess.CmdRun goroutine in pkg/daemon/pmc.go (introduced in PR #617) interferes with BC's dual ptp4l instance startup. Check daemon logs on the BC node during config deployment for any panic, socket error, or race condition preventing ptp4l.1.config from starting.

• Group 3: phc2sys SIGTERM test — new test failure (affects: "Should restart phc2sys with no socket errors after SIGTERM")
• Evidence:

```

Expected phc2sys process status to be 1 for GM

Expected : 0 to equal : 1

Timed out after 180.001s

```

• Root Cause: phc2sys process count is 0 (not running) when the test expects 1 (running after SIGTERM-triggered restart). This test was added in ptp-operator PR #251 (Jul 1, 2026, "tests-cleanup-ptp-socket-and-processes"). Since the BC is not in LOCKED state (Group 2), phc2sys may not be started by the daemon in the first place, making the SIGTERM-and-count test impossible to pass. It is also possible the new test itself has a timing or node-targeting bug.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/459caf2a4caca4da18bad618e0674eee1f5b4044 (PR #251) and/or the Group 2 daemon regression
• Is it a flake? No
• Suggested Fix: Fix Group 2 (BC sync) first; if the test still fails with a synced BC, inspe



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

```

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/alias.GetAlias, neither of which maps it to the actual slave interface, so openshift_ptp_clock_state is labeled iface="master". The old extractRegularMetrics() code (metrics.go:473–475) had explicit logic to resolve "master" to the tracked slave interface alias via masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias. That resolution is absent from the new path.

• Breaking PR/Commit: fd6ff1e — https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/commit/fd6ff1e2dc9c651a96a85a976da01498fa0b3417 (Merge PR #617 "upstream-sync-2026-06-30", merged 2026-06-30). The specific bug lives in the newly created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics().

• Is it a flake? No — the metric label regression is deterministic on any run using the main branch of linuxptp-daemon after this merge.

• Suggested Fix: In processParsedMetrics() for the ptp4lProcessName case, when ptpMetrics.Iface resolves to "master", fall back to masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias before calling updateClockStateMetrics — mirroring the logic in the old extractRegularMetrics() at metrics.go:473. Also remove (or guard) the masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) call on line 118 when Iface is "master" to prevent corrupting the tracked slave interface.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (10 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The BC's upstream ptp4l instance (ptp4l.1.config / ens2f0np0) is stuck in MASTER state rather than SLAVE, blocking clock synchronization entirely, compounded by a broken pmc fallback command introduced in a June 2026 ptp-operator commit.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Broken pmc command args in getClockIDViaPMC fallback (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

grandmasterIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config: bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

• Root Cause: Commit 3b93c9f (Jun 11, 2026, "tests: add retries to PTP clock ID lookups") introduced getClockIDViaPMC in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 which passes "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" as two separate arguments to pmc via pods.ExecCommand. In pmc's batch mode, each argv[optind++] is fed independently to pmc_do_command() (see pmc_common.c:1070), which calls sscanf(str, " %10s %64s", action_str, id_str). Passing "GET" alone returns only 1 match (not 2), so pmc_do_command returns -1 and prints "bad command: GET". Then "PARENT_DATA_SET" alone similarly produces "bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET". The correct invocation passes "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single argument so that sscanf parses both tokens in one call. The log-based lookup (1-minute timeout) fails first because the BC is in FREERUN, triggering this broken fallback.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/3b93c9f408647a8b55d37920103c57a3e2cb11c2 (ptphelper.go:144 — []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} must be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"})
• Is it a flake? No — deterministic, every run where log parsing falls back to pmc will fail identically
• Suggested Fix: Change the ExecCommand call in getClockIDViaPMC to pass "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single slice element, matching the working pattern already used in ptptesthelper.go:588 ([]string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}).

• Group 2: BC ptp4l.1.config stuck in FREERUN / ens2f0np0 in MASTER (affects: "Verify clockClass when locking PTP source on dual NIC boundary clock", "PTP metric is present verifies on slave", "Should receive events after linuxptp-daemon pod restart", "The slave node network interface is taken down and up", "BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage")
• Evidence:

```

Expected PMC clock class 6 for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config

Expected : 248 to equal : 6


incorrect role for ens2f0np0, role expected=1(SLAVE), role observed=2(MASTER)


Time metrics are not detected


timedout PushInitialValue, waiting for log in ns=ptp-event-framework-test pod=consumer,

looking for = Got CurrentState: ({.*})

```

From the linuxptp-daemon log on cnfdf32 (the BC node):

```

ptp4l[22542.578]: [ptp4l.0.config:5] port 1 (eno12399np0): LISTENING to MASTER on ANNOUNCE_RECEIPT_TIMEOUT_EXPIRES

ptp4l[22542.578]: [ptp4l.0.config:5] selected local clock b49691.fffe.cc0964 as best master

```

— No ptp4l.1.config entries present in the cnfdf32 daemon log spanning the entire test window.

• Root Cause: The BC's upstream ptp4l instance (ptp4l.1.config / ens2f0np0 on cnfdf32) never reaches SLAVE state because it does not receive PTP Announce messages from the GM, causing ANNOUNCE_RECEIPT_TIMEOUT_EXPIRES and self-promotion to MASTER. Clock class therefore stays at 248 (FREERUN). The absence of ptp4l.1.config entries in the cnfdf32 daemon log (7792-line, 976 KB log spanning the full test window) despite ptp4l.0.config running normally on the same pod suggests the BC's second ptp4l instance either did not start or was never configured to start. The most likely candidate is a regression in linuxptp-daemon main branch commit fd6ff1e (PR #617, "Sync from upstream 30-Jun-2026"), which added new PMCProcess management code in pkg/daemon/pmc.go (328 net-new lines) that could interfere with multi-config BC startup sequencing — though the exact daemon-side mechanism cannot be pinpointed without the cnfdf31 full log. The same symptom (ens2f0np0 in MASTER, clockClass 248) reproduces identically in the dualnicbcha suite on the same run, indicating a systemic BC configuration failure rather than a transient flake.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/pull/617 (fd6ff1e) — "Sync from upstream 2026-06-30" on main branch. Needs further investigation with full cnfdf31 daemon logs. Verified main-branch-only (check_backport would need to confirm absence on release-5.0); however, since USE_UPSTREAM=true this run, images are built from main directly.
• Is it a flake? No — persists for the entire suite duration (>1 hour) and reproduces across two back-to-back suites (dualnicbc and dualnicbcha)
• Suggested Fix: Investigate whether the new PMCProcess.CmdRun goroutine in pkg/daemon/pmc.go (introduced in PR #617) interferes with BC's dual ptp4l instance startup. Check daemon logs on the BC node during config deployment for any panic, socket error, or race condition preventing ptp4l.1.config from starting.

• Group 3: phc2sys SIGTERM test — new test failure (affects: "Should restart phc2sys with no socket errors after SIGTERM")
• Evidence:

```

Expected phc2sys process status to be 1 for GM

Expected : 0 to equal : 1

Timed out after 180.001s

```

• Root Cause: phc2sys process count is 0 (not running) when the test expects 1 (running after SIGTERM-triggered restart). This test was added in ptp-operator PR #251 (Jul 1, 2026, "tests-cleanup-ptp-socket-and-processes"). Since the BC is not in LOCKED state (Group 2), phc2sys may not be started by the daemon in the first place, making the SIGTERM-and-count test impossible to pass. It is also possible the new test itself has a timing or node-targeting bug.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator/commit/459caf2a4caca4da18bad618e0674eee1f5b4044 (PR #251) and/or the Group 2 daemon regression
• Is it a flake? No
• Suggested Fix: Fix Group 2 (BC sync) first; if the test still fails with a synced BC, inspe
